children.text: for the benefit of Gallaudet University or for the construction of facilities for its use; or
for the benefit of the National Technical Institute for the Deaf or for the construction of facilities for its use,

children.heading: Independent financial and compliance audit
children.label: b Independent financial and compliance audit

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Gallaudet University shall have an annual independent financial and compliance audit made of the programs and activities of the University, including the national mission and school operations of the elementary and secondary education programs at Gallaudet. The institution of higher education with which the Secretary has an agreement under shall have an annual independent financial and compliance audit made of the programs and activities of such institution of higher education, including NTID, and containing specific schedules and analyses for all NTID funds, as determined by the Secretary.section 4332 of this title

children.heading: Compliance
children.label: 2 Compliance
children.text: As used in paragraph (1), compliance means compliance with sections 4302(b), 4305(b)(4), 4332(b)(5), 4353(c), 4357(b)(2) of this title, subsections (c) through (f) of , and subsections (a), (b), and (c) of .1section 4357 of this titlesection 4359a of this titleSo in original. Probably should be followed by “and”.1

children.heading: Submission of audits
children.label: 3 Submission of audits
children.text: A copy of each audit described in paragraph (1) shall be provided to the Secretary and the Committee on Education and Labor of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate within 15 days of acceptance of the audit by the University or the institution authorized to establish and operate the NTID under , as the case may be, but not later than January 10 of each year.section 4332(a) of this title

children.heading: Limitations regarding expenditure of funds
children.label: c Limitations regarding expenditure of funds
A
children.text: Alcoholic beverages.

children.label: B
children.text: Goods or services for personal use.

children.label: C
children.text: Housing and personal living expenses (but only to the extent such expenses are not required by written employment agreement).

children.label: D
children.text: Lobbying, except that nothing in this subparagraph shall be construed to prohibit the University and NTID from educating the Congress, the Secretary, and others regarding programs, projects, and activities conducted at those institutions.

children.label: E
children.text: Membership in country clubs and social or dining clubs and organizations.

children.label: A
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the University and NTID shall develop policies, to be applied uniformly, for the allowability of expenditures for each institution. These policies should reflect the unique nature of these institutions. The principles established by the Office of Management and Budget for costs of educational institutions may be used as guidance in developing these policies. General principles relating to allowability and reasonableness of all costs associated with the operations of the institutions shall be addressed. These policies shall be submitted to the Secretary for review and comments, and to the Committee on Education and Labor of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate.October 16, 1992

children.label: i
children.text: Noninstitutional professional activities.

children.label: ii
children.text: Fringe benefits.

children.label: iii
children.text: Interest on loans.

children.label: iv
children.text: Rental cost of buildings and equipment.

children.label: v
children.text: Sabbatical leave.

children.label: vi
children.text: Severance pay.

children.label: vii
children.text: Travel.

children.label: viii
children.text: Royalties and other costs for uses of patents.

children.label: C
children.text: The Secretary is not authorized to add items to those specified in subparagraph (B).
